DISCOVERY, 302, AS TO PLAINTIFF ALYCESUN DALEY'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AND TESTIMONY AT DESPOITIONS, THE COURT REFERS THIS MATTER TO A DISCOVERY REFEREE FOR AN IN CAMERA REVIEW OF DOCUMENTS AND CASE MANAGEMENT OF DISCOVERY. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE SELECTION OF THE DISCOVERY REFEREE. IF PARTIES REACH AN AGREEMENT, PARTIES ARE TO FILE A STIPULATION WITH THE COURT. THE COURT SETS A HEARING IN THIS CASE ON 11/20/15 AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPT. 302, DISCOVERY, FOR A STATUS CONFERENCE REGARDING THE SELECTION OF THE DISCOVERY REFEREE. IF THERE IS A STIPULATION ON FILE PRIOR TO THE 11/20/15 HEARING, THE MATTER WILL BE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR, AND PARTIES NEED NOT APPEAR. THE COURT SHALL PREPARE A FORMAL ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: KATHERINE GALLO, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Pro Hac Vice Application Of Jeffrey Killino
Off Calendar Apr-26-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEFFREY B. KILLINO OF THE KILLINO FFIRM, P.C., TEL. NO. 1-877-875-2927 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. FRANCES BURNS OF DONNELLY NELSON DEPOLO & MURAY, TEL. NO. 1-925-287-8181 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Alycesun Daley's application for the pro hac vice admission of Jeffrey Killino is granted subject to review if the State Bar chooses to provide information on this application. This application arises out of Mr. Killino's failure to pay the renewal fee for the pro hac vice admission he received earlier in this case. Because Mr. Killino has served as Ms. Daley's counsel since Mr. Killino's first pro hac vice application was granted and denial of this application would likely impose a significant hardship on Ms. Daley and impair her choice of counsel, this application should only be denied on a strong showing that do so is necessary to protect the interests of Ms. Daley, the Regents, this court or the public. While the opposition papers raise some troubling issues about Mr. Killino's ethics and civility, there has been no showing that this court has been unable to address any asserted misconduct by Mr. Killino in this case. Nor has there been any showing that Ms. Daley, the Regents or this court has been prejudiced by Mr. Killino being allowed to represent Ms. Daley in this case. Without further information the court is not able to assess the assertion that Mr. Killino and his firm have in the past or are currently violating any rules regarding advertising or publicity in California or any other rules established by the State Bar or the Legislature for the protection of the public. The court will review this application if the State Bar chooses to provide any information on these issues. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion To Revoke Jeffrey Killinos Pro Hac Vice Status
Off Calendar Jun-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica MacGregor of Long & Levit LLP telephone number 415-397-2222 specially appearing for Attorney Jeffrey Killino on behalf of Plaintiff Alycesun Daley.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, DIANA FARMER, HANMIN LEE and ROBERT BALL'S Motion To Revoke Jeffrey Killinos Pro Hac Vice Status is denied. The Regents have not demonstrated that counsel Killino, personally, regularly practices law in California or is engaged in substantial business, professional or other activities in the State of California such that he is ineligible for pro hac vice admission for purposes of representing Plaintiff Alycesun Daley in this action. (See Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 9.40(a)(2)-(3).) Nor do the "additional facts" presented by The Regents support disqualification of Killino or revocation of his pro hac vice status. Killino's request for sanctions under Code of Civil Procedure section 128.5 is denied. Order signed in open Court.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear at hearing. Both sides stipulate to the matter being by the judge pro tem. Matter argued by counsel. DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, DIANA FARMER, HAMLIN LEE AND ROBERT BALL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety.
